req.title: KYC refresh (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-040 mandates KYC refresh every 365 days with account restriction beyond the window. Key test dimensions: (1) EP positive — customer within refresh window submits valid KYC and becomes verified; (2) NEG negative — submission with non-compliant data yields 403 and verified stays false; (3) BVA boundary at exactly day 364 (last valid day, should succeed); (4) BVA boundary at exactly day 365 (first restricted day, outbound blocked); (5) STC state transition — account moves from restricted to verified after successful refresh. BVA is critical per the requirement's explicit boundary-behaviour mandate. Each test uses a fixture to provide an authenticated session and a helper to seed account age, keeping executable code within 40 lines.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer account last KYC refresh was 180 days ago (well within 365-day window)
KYC form payload is fully compliant
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with compliant KYC payload and valid auth header
Assert response body contains customer.verified=true and refreshed timestamp
expected: Response JSON: refreshed field is a recent ISO-8601 timestamp
trace: R-FIN-040: customer.verified=true refreshed on successful submission
EP: selects representative value from valid-submission equivalence class (day 180)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime

def test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window(auth_session, seed_account_age, api_base_url):
    seed_account_age(days_since_kyc=180)
    payload = <compliant_kyc_payload>()
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert "refreshed" in body["customer"]
    refreshed_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(body["customer"]["refreshed"])
    assert (datetime.utcnow() - refreshed_dt).total_seconds() < 60

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_non_compliant_payload_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer account last KYC refresh was 30 days ago
KYC form payload contains non-compliant data (e.g. missing mandatory field)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with non-compliant KYC payload and valid auth header
Assert customer.verified is not set to true in any error body
expected: Response body indicates compliance failure reason
trace: R-FIN-040: error pathway — 403 when submission fails compliance
NEG: exercises the invalid-submission partition to confirm rejection
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_non_compliant_payload_returns_403(auth_session, seed_account_age, api_base_url):
    seed_account_age(days_since_kyc=30)
    payload = <non_compliant_kyc_payload>()
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    verified = body.get("customer", {}).get("verified", False)
    assert verified is not True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_364_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer account last KYC refresh was exactly 364 days ago (one day before window expires)
KYC form payload is fully compliant
expected: HTTP 200 OK — day 364 is still within the 365-day window
Account remains fully functional (outbound transactions permitted)
trace: R-FIN-040: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds
BVA: upper boundary minus one — day 364 is last safe interior point
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_364_succeeds(auth_session, seed_account_age, api_base_url):
    seed_account_age(days_since_kyc=364)
    payload = <compliant_kyc_payload>()
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_365_account_restricted_to_inbound_only
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer account last KYC refresh was exactly 365 days ago (first day beyond window)
Account restriction endpoint is accessible at /accounts/{id}/permissions
actions: GET /accounts/{id}/permissions to confirm outbound is restricted before refresh
GET /accounts/{id}/permissions again to confirm restriction is lifted
expected: Before refresh: outbound_enabled == false, inbound_enabled == true
POST /kyc/refresh returns HTTP 200 and customer.verified == true
trace: R-FIN-040: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound only; boundary at day 365
BVA: upper boundary — exact threshold where restriction activates
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_365_account_restricted_to_inbound_only(
    auth_session, seed_account_age, api_base_url, account_id
):
    seed_account_age(days_since_kyc=365)
    perms_before = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/permissions")
    assert perms_before.status_code == 200
    before = perms_before.json()
    assert before["outbound_enabled"] is False
    assert before["inbound_enabled"] is True
    payload = <compliant_kyc_payload>()
    refresh_resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True
    perms_after = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/permissions")
    assert perms_after.json()["outbound_enabled"] is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_restricted_to_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer account is currently in RESTRICTED state (kyc_status=restricted)
Customer account last KYC refresh was 400 days ago
actions: GET /kyc/status to confirm initial state is restricted
GET /kyc/status to confirm final state is verified
expected: POST /kyc/refresh returns HTTP 200 with customer.verified == true
trace: R-FIN-040: expected outcome customer.verified=true refreshed after submission
STC: covers RESTRICTED -> VERIFIED state transition triggered by successful refresh
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_state_transition_restricted_to_verified(
    auth_session, seed_account_age, api_base_url
):
    seed_account_age(days_since_kyc=400)
    status_before = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/status")
    assert status_before.status_code == 200
    assert status_before.json()["kyc_status"] == "restricted"
    payload = <compliant_kyc_payload>()
    refresh_resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/refresh", json=payload)
    assert refresh_resp.status_code == 200
    assert refresh_resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True
    status_after = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/kyc/status")
    assert status_after.status_code == 200
    assert status_after.json()["kyc_status"] == "verified"
